tools.name: check_availability
tools.description: List available cleaning slots. Saturdays and Sundays only, 8 AM – 6 PM PT, San Francisco only. Omit `date` for the next 8 upcoming weekend days.

tools.name: initiate_booking
tools.description: Reserve a cleaning slot. No payment is collected up front — the customer pays the cleaner in cash or card at the appointment. Returns `{ status: "booked" }`, the slot is locked in the calendar, and a calendar invite is sent to the email. Always ask the customer for full details (date, start time, ho

tools.name: check_booking_status
tools.description: List upcoming bookings for a customer by email. Queries Google Calendar directly, so results are live and reflect any bookings in the next ~20 events.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "initiate_booking" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Reserve a cleaning slot. No payment is collected up front — the customer pays the cleaner in cash or card at the appointment. Returns `{ status: "booked" }`, th…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_booking_status · Public sink: initiate_booking, check_booking_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_booking_status · Public sink: initiate_booking, check_booking_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
